name: DeepSpeed On Slurm
summary: Plan and smoke test DeepSpeed launches on Slurm GPU allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm wrapper for DeepSpeed jobs, including environment capture, storage and checkpoint reminders, a reviewable launch plan, and an optional tiny DeepSpeed smoke test.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Used to derive MASTER_ADDR from Slurm node lists when available.

tools.name: deepspeed
tools.notes: Required for smoke tests and real training, but not for plan-only mode.

inputs.name: gpu_partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue that provides GPU nodes.

inputs.name: nodes
inputs.description: Number of nodes to allocate for the smoke test or training job.

inputs.name: gpus_per_node
inputs.description: Number of GPUs requested per node.

inputs.name: deepspeed_config
inputs.description: DeepSpeed JSON configuration file to review or use.

inputs.name: run_deepspeed_smoke
inputs.description: Opt-in flag that runs a tiny DeepSpeed smoke test.

outputs.name: launch_plan
outputs.description: Captured Slurm layout, environment evidence, and reviewable DeepSpeed launch command.

outputs.name: deepspeed_smoke_report
outputs.description: Optional import, distributed initialization, and one-step tiny model training report.

outputs.name: storage_checklist
outputs.description: Checkpoint, scratch, log, and data staging reminders for large model training.

examples.title: Plan or run a DeepSpeed smoke test on Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run plan-only mode on a login node or short GPU allocation, then set RUN_DEEPSPEED_SMOKE=1 on a debug GPU partition with a known DeepSpeed environment.
